action.reasoning: Observation: Pure spinning causes robot to drift away (dist 2.93→9.88m) because human walks away. The spin direction oscillates at ±π boundary. Analysis: Need to CHASE the human while turning. When human is behind, do a forward circle with committed direction. The circle radius = lin_vel/ang_vel. With lin_vel=8, ang_vel=15, radius≈0.53m (tight circle). Need to commit to direction for many steps to avoid oscillation. Also need to handle the case where human is moving away - must maintain forward motion. Decision: Rewrite predict.py with: 1) When |angle| > π/2: forward circle with committed direction (50 steps), 2) When |angle| < π/2: approach/maintain with distance control, 3) Stuck detection based on distance not changing. Also add navmesh-based approach for long-range navigation.
